title: The Hermaphrodite Child of the Sun and Moon (Hermetic Triumph / Hermetic Romance)
subtitle: The natural-supernatural birth, destruction, and regeneration of the symbolic Child — theory and practice of the Philosopher's Stone in 12 emblems

summary.one_line: An anonymous 1752 German work presenting the alchemical opus as the hierogamy of Sol and Luna producing the hermaphroditic Child, with 12 emblematic figures and Qabalistic signs of Solomon.
summary.scope: The text analyzes the conjunction of opposites, the death and regeneration of the alchemical child, the role of the hermaphrodite as the rebis, and the Qabalistic seals of Solomon applied to the work.

sources.label: Hermaphroditisches Sonn- und Monds-Kind (Anonymous, 1752; tr. Mike Brenner, 1997)

body.text: THE HERMAPHRODITE CHILD OF THE SUN AND MOON. Abstract: Analyzes the natural and supernatural birth, destruction, and regeneration of the symbolic Child. Introduces the theory and practice of how to quest for and manufacture the Philosopher's Stone. Includes applications and explanations of verses by the famous Swedish Adept Norton, from an ancient manuscript. Now, for the first time, revealed according to mystical tradition and esoteric enlightenment. Includes the qabbalistic signs of Solomon, explained by a master-student of nature. With 12 emblematical figures with corresponding explanatory paragraphs. (Title page summary)

The hermaphrodite Child is the alchemical rebis — the 'thing of two things' that results from the coniunctio oppositorum. In Layayoga, this is the union of pingala and ida in sushumna, producing the ardhanarishvara state where Shiva and Shakti are one. The Tantraloka describes this as the 'samarasa' state of equal taste, where dualities dissolve in the supreme consciousness. Steiner's Rose Cross meditation (the black cross with red roses) produces a similar hermaphroditic spiritual body — the 'sheathing' that results from the union of the astral with the etheric. In daimon work, the hermaphrodite is the Holy Guardian Angel itself: the daimon is neither masculine nor feminine but the androgyne of the soul, the 'perfect nature' (tabiat-e tammah) of Suhrawardi's Illuminationist philosophy.

Analyses the natural and supernatural birth, destruction, and regeneration of the symbolic Child. Introduces the theory and practice of how to quest for and manufacture the Philosopher's Stone. The Child is born of the Sun and Moon, destroyed (mortified, putrefied) in the philosophical egg, and regenerated as the Lapis — the stone that is no stone, the fixed and volatile made one.

The three-stage cycle of birth-destruction-regeneration is the alchemical version of the daimon path's systasis: the practitioner is 'born' as a separate ego, 'destroyed' through the Saturn retreat (the mortification of the false self), and 'regenerated' as the HGA-union. In PGM terms, this is the skinning (birth), the egg purification (destruction), and the communion (regeneration). The Tantraloka's diksha (initiation) follows the same arc: the guru kills the old self and births the new. Steiner's Mystery of Golgotha is the macrocosmic version: Christ dies (destruction) and rises (regeneration) as the cosmic hermaphrodite uniting heaven and earth. The 'philosophical egg' is the akasha — the daimonic womb where the transformation occurs.

Includes the qabbalistic signs of Solomon, explained by a master-student of nature. With 12 emblematical figures with corresponding explanatory paragraphs. (Title page summary; individual emblems in herm01.html through herm12.html)

The Qabalistic signs of Solomon are the magical seals that govern the four worlds (Atziluth, Beriah, Yetzirah, Assiah) as applied to the alchemical work. In PGM, these correspond to the characteres and voces magicae that compel daimonic obedience. The 12 emblems map to the 12 stages of the zodiacal year — the solar path through the houses, which in Layayoga corresponds to the 12 phases of prana through the chakras. Steiner's 12-fold division of the zodiac as 'spiritual hierarchies' in manifestation provides the anthroposophical key: each emblem is a hieroglyph of a planetary-spiritual force. The daimon path sees these 12 as the 'gates' through which the daimon descends into incarnation — the PGM systasis opens each gate in sequence.
